mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are non-negative in more than 60% of contraction periods, or the signal shows no predictive power during documented supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the threshold forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 50% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with quarterly equity returns in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining GDP growth and falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand collapse, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in industrial production indicates immediate capacity utilization and earnings upside, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of supply chain disruption.
